question: A process instance is active at a service task whose job was created with type `send_mail`. The target process definition changes the job type expression to the one shown below. After migrating the instance, what is the resulting job type?

options.text: `send_mail`, because existing jobs keep their properties; the new job type expression is not re-evaluated during migration.
The result of evaluating the new expression at migration time, assigned as the job's new type.
Empty, because the job is cancelled when the service task is mapped to the new definition.
Both `send_mail` and the new value, with workers able to subscribe to either type after migration.

a.text: Migration does not recreate jobs, re-evaluate expressions, or re-apply input mappings on active elements. The job keeps its original properties, including the job type set when it was first activated.

b.text: The new expression is not evaluated at migration time. To pick it up, you must terminate the active service task and re-activate it via process instance modification.

c.text: Migration does not cancel the existing job. The job continues to exist with the same key and type as before migration.

d.text: A job has a single job type. The original `send_mail` type remains; no second job type is introduced by migration.

explanation: Process instance migration does not recreate jobs, reevaluate expressions, or reapply input mappings on active elements. A job that was created with type `send_mail` keeps that exact type after migration, even if the target definition's job type expression differs. To pick up the new expression you would terminate and re-activate the task via process instance modification.
